prompt: Recommend three works of fiction that you think every thoughtful adult should read at some point. Explain each in two or three sentences.
response:
1. One Hundred Years of Solitude by Gabriel García Márquez - This masterpiece of magical realism tells the multi-generational story of the Buendía family in the fictional town of Macondo. It explores themes of love, war, politics, and the cyclical nature of time with profound wisdom and poetic beauty.

2. The Brothers Karamazov by Fyodor Dostoevsky - This sprawling novel delves deep into questions of morality, free will, and the existence of God through the lives of the four Karamazov brothers. Dostoevsky probes the human condition with unparalleled psychological insight and spiritual urgency.  

3. Invisible Man by Ralph Ellison - Centered around an unnamed African American narrator, this novel offers a powerful meditation on race, identity, and the struggle for self-definition in America. Ellison wields a visionary imagination to expose the ways black men have been rendered "invisible" in a white-dominated society, while also crafting an epic, mythic tale that transcends time and place.
